title: Upon Brock. Epig.
content:
To cleanse his eyes, Tom Brock makes much ado,
    But not his mouth, the fouler of the two.
    A clammy rheum makes loathsome both his eyes:
    His mouth, worse furr'd with oaths and blasphemies.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Upon Brock. Epig."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
